# Recall OS

> Recall OS is a local-first, architecture-neutral CLI that turns a repository into durable,
> AI-ready engineering memory — the *why* behind the code — and validates that memory with a
> deterministic `recall doctor`. No network calls, no telemetry, no AI API calls, no code generation.

Recall OS writes structured, reviewable memory (product intent, architecture decisions, module
ownership, testing and security expectations, AI agent rules) into plain files that live next to the
code and survive every session boundary. The agent does the semantic thinking; Recall OS keeps the
memory and checks the evidence. It generates agent skills and a SessionStart hook so a fresh AI
session reliably loads that memory.

Key principles for an agent reading this project:

- Repository memory in `docs/` is the durable source of truth; chat history is not.
- Recall OS is architecture-neutral: presets and adopt only *propose* decisions; a human accepts
  them with `recall adr accept`. Never encode a stack choice as accepted truth on your own.
- Repository rules override model preferences. If sources conflict, stop and report the conflict.
- `recall doctor` is deterministic, offline, and read-only — it checks that memory is present,
  consistent, and evidenced, not whether the architecture is good.

## Start here

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/README.md): What it is, install, quickstart, commands, and what Doctor checks.
- [Philosophy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/PHILOSOPHY.md): Why it exists and the deliberate bet on discipline over magic.
- [Recall Commands](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/ai/RECALL_COMMANDS.md): Every CLI command, options, and the completion gate.
- [AI Agent Rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/60-engineering/AI_AGENT_RULES.md): How an agent must behave in a Recall OS repository.

## Architecture

- [Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/10-architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md): System design and the deterministic CLI pipeline.
- [Memory Engine](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/10-architecture/MEMORY_ENGINE.md): The repository memory model and source-of-truth ordering.
- [Opinion Packs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/10-architecture/OPINION_PACKS.md): How presets propose stack decisions without accepting them.
- [File Write Policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/10-architecture/FILE_WRITE_POLICY.md): Non-destructive, skip-existing, force-to-overwrite write rules.
- [Repository Decisions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/10-architecture/REPOSITORY_DECISIONS.md): Repo-level decisions that override model preference.

## AI agents, skills, and MCP

- [AI Agents, Skills, and MCP Strategy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/ai/AI_AGENTS_SKILLS_MCP_STRATEGY.md): How agent entry points, skills, and MCP relate to durable memory.
- [MCP Strategy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/ai/MCP_STRATEGY.md): Why MCP context is captured offline as proposed memory.
- [Skill Authoring Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/ai/SKILL_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md): How the generated, scriptless agent skills are structured.
- [Module Delivery Workflow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/ai/MODULE_DELIVERY_WORKFLOW.md): The mini product workflow each module follows.

## Quality and engineering

- [Quality Gates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/50-quality/QUALITY_GATES.md): The completion-evidence gate and required checks.
- [Engineering Standards](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/60-engineering/ENGINEERING_STANDARDS.md): Baseline rules that repository memory enforces.
- [Testing Strategy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/50-quality/TESTING_STRATEGY.md): How tests derive from acceptance, risk, and boundaries.
- [Definition of Done](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/50-quality/DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md): What "complete" requires before it can be claimed.

## Security

- [Security Model](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/20-security/SECURITY_MODEL.md): The local-first, no-network, no-secret-handling stance.
- [Threat Model](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/20-security/THREAT_MODEL.md): Risks the tool defends against (path traversal, symlinks, overwrites).
- [Privacy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/20-security/PRIVACY.md): What never leaves the machine.

## Decisions (accepted ADRs)

- [ADR-0001: Deterministic placeholder renderer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0001-deterministic-placeholder-renderer.md)
- [ADR-0002: Pre-commit hook generation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0002-pre-commit-hook-generation.md)
- [ADR-0003: recall adopt proposes, not accepts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0003-recall-adopt-proposes-not-accepts.md)
- [ADR-0004: Skill generation is portable and scriptless](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0004-skill-generation-portable-and-scriptless.md)
- [ADR-0005: MCP context memory is proposed and offline](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0005-mcp-context-memory-is-proposed-and-offline.md)
- [ADR-0006: adr accept promotes and removes the proposal](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0006-adr-accept-promotes-and-removes-the-proposal.md)

- [Product Vision](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/00-product/PRODUCT_VISION.md): Where Recall OS is headed.
- [Positioning](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/docs/00-product/POSITIONING.md): How it differs from templates, RAG, and hosted memory.
- [Contributing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): How to contribute, including adding a preset.
- [Security Policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/recall-os/main/SECURITY.md): How to report vulnerabilities.
